title: Hook tests — fabric-hint.cjs KB: capture + event emission (parseKbLine + extractAndWriteAssistantTurnsBestEffort)
description: Targeted hook-side tests for the cite-line parser + event emitter added in TASK-03. Uses fixture transcript JSONL files + fixture stdin payloads. Verifies: parseKbLine handles all tag enum + multi-cite + dismissed reason + 'KB: none'; extractAndWriteAssistantTurnsBestEffort produces valid event lines parseable by Zod schema from TASK-02; hook never throws on malformed input (silent exit). If existing hook-test infrastructure absent, create minimal harness mirroring banner-i18n.test.cjs / installer-test style.

files.change: Test file structure: (1) Helper `loadHint(env)` requires fabric-hint.cjs with mocked stdio (override tryReadStdinJson to return fixture payload); (2) Helper `mkTranscript(envelopes)` writes a fixture JSONL to tmpdir and returns path; (3) Tests: parseKbLine 'planned' tag, parseKbLine 'recalled' tag, parseKbLine 'chained-from KP-001' tag, parseKbLine 'dismissed:scope-mismatch', parseKbLine 'dismissed:other:custom', parseKbLine 'KB: none', parseKbLine multi-cite comma-separated, parseKbLine empty/malformed input silent default. (4) Tests: extractAndWriteAssistantTurnsBestEffort with valid Claude Code transcript + assistant envelopes emits N event lines to .fabric/events.jsonl; each line passes JSON.parse + matches assistantTurnObservedEventSchema (require Zod schema from @fenglimg/fabric-shared, parse each emitted line). (5) Tests: malformed transcript_path → silent no-op (no throw, no events written). (6) Tests: missing stdinPayload → silent no-op. (7) Tests: client detection: when path includes '.claude/' → client='cc'; '.codex/' → client='codex'.

implementation: Check if packages/cli/templates/hooks/ has any existing .test.cjs / .test.ts files (e.g. banner-i18n.test.cjs per recent rc.16 work).

If existing harness: extend it. If absent: create packages/cli/test/hooks/fabric-hint.test.ts using vitest or whatever test runner the cli package uses (check packages/cli/package.json for test runner).
Implement loadHint helper: dynamic require with stubbed stdio (process.stdin override or env var pointing to fixture).
Implement mkTranscript: write tmp JSONL file with array of envelope-shaped objects ({type:'user'|'assistant', content:[...], message:{role,content}}).
Write 8 parseKbLine tests covering all tag enum + edge cases.
Write 5 extractAndWriteAssistantTurnsBestEffort tests covering happy path + 3 silent-failure paths + Zod-schema roundtrip validation.
Write 2 client detection tests (path heuristic + FABRIC_HINT_CLIENT env var override).
If hook test infrastructure is brittle or requires significant setup, document in commit and propose follow-up RC.

reference.pattern: Hook script unit testing via dynamic require + fixture stdio
reference.examples: rc.16 F2-tests added banner-i18n coverage — same harness pattern applies.

rationale.chosen_approach: Isolated unit tests on the pure helper (parseKbLine) + integration-ish tests on extractAndWriteAssistantTurnsBestEffort (fixture transcript → assert events.jsonl content). Cross-validate emitted events against shared Zod schema from TASK-02 to lock the hook↔schema contract.
alternatives_considered: Skip hook tests, rely on server-side tests only (rejected: server tests assume valid events.jsonl, can't catch hook-side parse bugs)
End-to-end test spawning a real Claude Code session (rejected: slow, environment-dependent, can't run on CI)
Inline tests inside fabric-hint.cjs itself (rejected: poisons production hook script with test code)
decision_factors: Parser is a pure function — easy to unit test
Hook never-block contract verifiable via malformed-input tests
Zod-schema roundtrip locks the hook↔server contract
rationale.tradeoffs: Hook tests are platform-sensitive (stdin/path heuristics); may require careful skip-on-Windows or env-var guards.

manual_checks: Run new hook test suite locally; confirm all tests pass
Intentionally regress parseKbLine (e.g. swap tag enum order) to confirm tests catch it
Run on Linux + macOS to confirm path heuristic works on both
success_metrics: Each emitted event line passes Zod schema validation per TASK-02
Hook never-throws contract verified via 3 malformed-input tests

criteria: All parseKbLine tag enum values covered (planned/recalled/chained-from/dismissed/none)
All dismissed reason enum values covered including 'other:<text>' suffix
Zod-schema roundtrip validation: each emitted event passes assistantTurnObservedEventSchema.parse
Hook never-throws contract verified: 3+ malformed-input tests assert silent exit + no events written
Client detection: cc + codex path heuristics covered + FABRIC_HINT_CLIENT env var override covered
Commit msg: `test(rc20): fabric-hint KB capture + event emit tests (TASK-09)`
